qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth2 dynamic registration permits low-privilege SSRF to loopback and internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an OAuth2 credential SSRF in `repo/packages/cli/src/credentials/*.ts` and `repo/packages/cli/src/oauth/*.ts`, while the advisory describes a Python Code Node / Python Task Runner sandbox escape that requires workflow-edit capability and Python Task Runner enablement. The affected entrypoints, component, and root cause do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's Python Task Runner sandbox-escape root cause. The only in-scope qualifying finding is a low-privilege OAuth2 dynamic-registration SSRF in the credentials and OAuth discovery flow, which is unrelated to Python Code Node sandbox isolation or task-runner escape.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution from Python Code Node workflows when the Python Task Runner is enabled.

The only session-produced qualifying finding targets `POST /credentials` and `GET /oauth2-credential/auth`, with code locations under `repo/packages/cli/src/credentials/*.ts` and `repo/packages/cli/src/oauth/*.ts`.

The stored proof artifact records only outbound loopback OAuth discovery and registration requests: `GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/resource`, `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/issuer`, and `POST /register`.
